answer: Manusmṛti 7.99 (corpus label Mn_7.99[100M]) prescribes a fourfold duty for a king regarding wealth/resources: (1) he should strive to obtain what has not been obtained; (2) protect with effort what has been obtained; (3) increase what has been protected; and (4) bestow what has been increased on worthy recipients. Found in work 'unknown-manusmrti-347b76', physically located in chapter 8 of the corpus (verses from chapter 7 continue there after the chapter split at paragraph 86).
